one_line_ask: Turn a dealer's raw, off-color bench photo of an aftermarket turbocharger into a clean color-accurate #FFFFFF ecommerce hero plus a transparent cutout, a scalable vector icon, and an outpainted 16:9 lifestyle banner, then collect them on a review board.
brief:
CONTEXT. We run a B2B ecommerce platform that lets automotive dealerships sell aftermarket performance parts online without carrying inventory. Our merchandising tone is operational, credible, and business-focused — NOT a flashy consumer ad. Dealers upload raw parts photos shot on a service-bay bench under mixed shop lighting; we need a repeatable Adobe pipeline that converts ONE such raw photo into a complete listing image pack that meets marketplace standards.

SUPPLIED ASSET (client). raw_turbo_bench.jpg — a single hand-shot photo of a chrome/aluminium aftermarket turbocharger sitting on a cluttered, slightly tilted workshop bench under warm fluorescent light (visibly off-white-balanced, mild underexposure, blown chrome highlights). Plus brand_palette.json carrying our hex palette (anchor accent #C8102E "performance red", neutral #1A1A1A) and a one-line note that listing backgrounds MUST be pure #FFFFFF.

WHAT WE NEED (deliverables).
1) PRIMARY HERO — a color-accurate, straightened, correctly exposed image of the turbo on a TRUE pure-white (#FFFFFF) background, cropped/resized to a 2048×2048 square (our listing-tile spec).
